headline: RM6.4B School Meal Programme Shows 34% Nutrient Deficiency
context: The Education Ministry's RM6.4 billion school feeding programme serving 3.2 million students was found by a 2025 Health Ministry audit to deliver meals meeting only 66% of recommended nutritional standards, with protein and vitamin deficiencies prevalent.

cards.big: RM6.4 billion feeding 3.2 million students — but 34% of meals fail basic nutritional standards per MOH audit
cards.sub: Average meal costs government RM4.50 but school canteen operators report receiving only RM2.80 per serving

cards.big: RM1.70 per meal lost between government allocation and plate — RM1.2 billion annually vanishes in supply chain markups
cards.sub: Centralised catering contracts awarded to 42 companies create oligopoly pricing that school-level vendors cannot challenge

cards.big: Child stunting in programme schools at 18.2% is actually higher than national average of 14.8%
cards.sub: Programme feeds children quantity but not quality — white rice with minimal protein is the standard daily offering

cards.big: No nutritionist involvement in menu planning at 78% of programme schools — menus designed by cost, not nutrition
cards.sub: Education Ministry oversees budget while Health Ministry sets standards — split responsibility means nobody is accountable for outcomes

cards.big: Spending RM6.4 billion to feed children meals that make them malnourished isn't a feeding programme — it's a spending programme
Should school meal contracts require mandatory nutritional audits with automatic termination for providers failing 2 consecutive assessments?
